perf-benchmark-skill grows with features; growth-audit deltas vs the pinned wave anchor are expected and managed by anchor policy, not code edits. Accepted as deferred-structural.

Incidental clone of the ~11-line JSONL-ledger parse loop (read lines, skip blanks, json.loads each, warn-and-skip on JSONDecodeError) shared by perf-benchmark's scripts/perf_benchmark/ledger.py::_read_ledger_entries and perf-optimization's perf-optimization/scripts/verify_win.py::_load_ledger_entries. The two live in SEPARATE, independently-installed skills (installed to distinct ~/.claude/skills dirs) that cannot import each other at runtime, so extracting a shared helper would break skill self-containment; each skill legitimately self-contains its own ledger reader. Surfaced only under the self-contained leaf set (jscpd via the pinned/cloned leaves), which is the canonical convergence env (CI convergence-gate). Line-pinned (L5): re-anchor if ledger.py lines 58-69 shift. Accepted as cross-skill-idiomatic.

perf-optimization/ is a separate, independently-installed skill not being version-bumped in this release; test file cold-path loop constructs in test_verify_win.py are not production code and the W8201 flags are test infrastructure. Accepted as separate-skill/test-cold-path.

perf-optimization/ is a separate, independently-installed skill not being version-bumped in this release; W8202 in select_candidate.py flags a module-level constant or loop-unpacked variable in the loop body. Accepted as separate-skill.

perf-optimization/ is a separate, independently-installed skill not being version-bumped in this release; W8202 in verify_win.py flags module-level constants or unpacked loop variables. Accepted as separate-skill.

perf-optimization/ is a separate, independently-installed skill not being version-bumped in this release; W8205 in verify_win.py flags dotted attribute access inside loops. Accepted as separate-skill.

perf-optimization/ is a separate, independently-installed skill not being version-bumped in this release; test file cold-path dotted-attribute access in test_verify_win.py loops is not production code. Accepted as separate-skill/test-cold-path.

perf-optimization/ is a separate, independently-installed skill not being version-bumped in this release; the for-loop accumulation in verify_win.py is more readable in the for-loop form. Accepted as separate-skill.

tests/test_accept_policy.py is a test cold-path; the W8201 flags in test loops (e.g., building expected structures for parametrize) are not production code. perflint over-approximates loop-invariant statements in test parametrize/fixture construction. Accepted as test-cold-path.
tests/test_accept_policy.py is a test cold-path; the W8202 flags reference module-level test constants inside test loops. Accepted as test-cold-path/over-approximation.
tests/test_profile_discover.py is a test cold-path; W8201 in test loops (e.g., iterating to check discovery results) is not production code. Accepted as test-cold-path.

tests/test_ledger.py is a test cold-path; the W8301 flag on a list literal in a test is not a production-path concern, and the list form is conventional in test parametrize/assertion constructs. Accepted as test-cold-path.
tests/test_pipeline_scoring_reporting.py is a test cold-path; the W8301 flag on a list literal in a test assertion or fixture is not a production-path concern. Accepted as test-cold-path.

benchmarks/bench_parse_massif.py is a benchmark cold-path, not production code; the W8301 flag on a list literal in a benchmark parametrize or setup is not a production concern. Accepted as benchmark-cold-path.

scripts/perf_benchmark/ledger.py W8201 at line 96: perflint flags the expression inside the _compare_dimensions for-loop over `dimensions` pairs, but the flagged expression accesses per-iteration dict values (loop-variant keys). This is a perflint over-approximation of loop-invariant hoisting. Accepted as over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/stage_helpers.py W8201 across multiple functions (_parse_cachegrind_summary, _parse_callgrind_output, _parse_perf_report, _parse_massif_out): perflint over-approximates loop-invariant hoisting on expressions that reference per-iteration parse state (e.g., match.group(), entry[header], part from zip). These are genuinely loop-variant. Accepted as over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/support.py W8201 at lines 88-92 and 97: perflint flags result[key]=... assignments inside the detect_cache_topology loop over indexes.values() where info is the loop variable; these are loop-variant subscript assignments, not invariant. Accepted as over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark_pipeline.py W8201 flags across run_parallel_tiers and stage_time: perflint over-approximates on expressions like {args.valgrind_timeout} and results[name] inside loops where name is the loop variable or where dict subscript depends on the loop key. Accepted as over-approximation.

scripts/profile_discover.py W8201 at line 27: perflint flags multiple occurrences of dict-construction inside the for (fname, lineno, func), (...) in raw_stats.items() loop where fname, lineno, func are the loop variables — these are genuinely loop-variant. Accepted as over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/findings.py W8202 at line 180: perflint flags reference to _PRESCRIPTIONS (a module-level constant dict) inside _suggested_action's for-loop. _PRESCRIPTIONS is a module-level constant mapping, not a mutable global; caching it in a local is a micro-optimization of negligible impact. Accepted as over-approximation on module-level constant.

scripts/perf_benchmark/scoring.py W8202 at lines 522 and 525: perflint flags references to TIER_RANK (a module-level constant dict) inside _detect_regressions's for-loop over dimensions. TIER_RANK is a module-level constant and caching it locally is a micro-optimization of negligible impact for this analysis path. Accepted as over-approximation on module-level constant.

scripts/perf_benchmark/support.py W8202 at line 199: perflint flags reference to _PYTEST_BENCHMARK_MARKER (a module-level compiled regex constant) inside a for-loop over py_file paths in _discover_pytest_benchmark_targets. This is a module-level constant; caching is a negligible micro-optimization. Accepted as over-approximation on module-level constant.

scripts/perf_benchmark_pipeline.py W8202 at lines 235, 251, 546, 626, 628, 631: perflint flags references to subprocess and DEFAULT_PERF_STAT_EVENTS module-level names inside loops. subprocess.TimeoutExpired and subprocess.run are module-attribute lookups that perflint suggests caching locally; for I/O-bound loops spawning external processes, the lookup overhead is negligible. Accepted as over-approximation on module-level names in I/O-dominated loops.

scripts/perf_benchmark/ledger.py W8205 at lines 63-64: perflint flags json.loads and json.JSONDecodeError inside the JSONL-parse loop in _read_ledger_entries. These are I/O-dominated file-reading loops where dotted attribute lookup overhead is negligible. The json.JSONDecodeError is used in an except clause where importing it directly would require a module-level import alias. Accepted as I/O-bound loop / over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/stage_helpers.py W8205 at lines 40, 188, 217, 239, 356, 361, 392: perflint flags re.search, re.match, and contextlib.suppress inside text-parsing loops. These are parse loops over profiler output text where regex call overhead dominates; the dotted lookup is negligible. contextlib.suppress is used as a context manager (must be called per-iteration). Accepted as I/O/parse-bound loop / over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark_pipeline.py W8205 at lines 237, 548, 627: perflint flags subprocess.run and subprocess.TimeoutExpired inside loops that spawn external processes (repeats of time/perf runs). These loops are I/O-dominated by subprocess execution; the dotted lookup overhead is negligible relative to process spawn cost. Accepted as I/O-dominated loop / over-approximation.

scripts/check_wave_baseline.py W8301 at line 33: _wave_command builds cmd as a list and then mutates it with += (lines 51-53) before returning it; converting to a tuple is not possible without restructuring. The list is also typed as list[str] in the return annotation. Accepted as mutated-list / type-annotation-constrained.

scripts/perf_benchmark/stage_helpers.py W8301 at line 65: the cmd_list built for the tracemalloc wrapper ([args.python, '-m', 'pytest', ...]) is typed as list[str] and consumed by _run_stage_command(cmd: list[str], ...). Converting to tuple would break the function signature. Accepted as type-annotation-constrained.

scripts/perf_benchmark_pipeline.py W8301 at lines 319, 366, 411, 461, 479, 567: all flagged lists are subprocess command lists passed to _run_stage_command(cmd: list[str], ...) or subprocess.run(). The helper is typed with cmd: list[str]; converting to tuples would violate the declared parameter type. These lists follow the conventional list[str] subprocess command pattern. Accepted as type-annotation-constrained / subprocess-command-convention.

scripts/perf_benchmark/reporting.py W8401 at line 271: _append_findings_section iterates dimension.items() in a nested for-loop body that calls lines.append(f'- **{key}**: {value}') — this is a side-effect pattern on an existing list (not building a new list), so a list comprehension would produce a discarded list of None values. The for-loop form is both correct and more readable. Accepted as for-loop-form-required / over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/scoring.py W8401 at line 110: _compute_complexity_exponent builds two parallel lists (matched_sizes and matched_times) by appending to both in the same loop body. A list comprehension cannot build two parallel lists simultaneously in one pass; the for-loop form is the only correct single-pass approach. Accepted as for-loop-form-required / over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/reporting.py W8402 at line 184: _append_algorithmic_result_section iterates missing_sub_checks to call lines.append(f'- `{name}`') with per-item formatting. This is not a list copy but a side-effect loop that formats and appends to lines; list.copy() or extend cannot apply per-item transformation. Accepted as for-loop-form-required / over-approximation.

scripts/profile_discover.py W8402 at line 25: _stats_to_rows iterates raw_stats.items() and calls rows.append({...dict...}) — this constructs a new dict per iteration from destructured tuple values; it is not a list copy but a transformation. The for-loop form is correct and readable. Accepted as for-loop-form-required / over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/reporting.py W8403 at line 481: _perf_record_summary builds perf_record_summary by conditionally inserting keys from perf_record in a for-loop with a conditional guard per key. A dict comprehension would be equivalent but the for-loop form is clearer for the conditional insertion pattern. Accepted as readable-for-loop-form.

scripts/perf_benchmark/stage_helpers.py W8403 at line 212: _parse_callgrind_output builds the functions list entry dict in a for-loop over zip(headers, parts) with contextlib.suppress(ValueError) wrapping each int() conversion — the exception suppressor cannot be embedded inside a dict comprehension value expression. Accepted as exception-suppression-required / over-approximation.

scripts/perf_benchmark/support.py W8403 at line 95: detect_cache_topology builds result by conditionally assigning D1/I1/LL keys from loop-iterated indexes.values() with multiple if/elif branches per iteration — a dict comprehension cannot express the multi-branch conditional accumulation pattern. Accepted as conditional-accumulation-required / over-approximation.
